title: Expose ontology-aware runtime input/status CLI for external supervisors
description:
## Context

`imm_supervisor` currently decides Codex/OMX state by scraping tmux pane text and injects prompts with local tmux logic. This creates false busy/idle decisions (for example, stale `Ran` lines below a now-idle `›` prompt) and duplicates ccbot runtime-input behavior.

The desired direction is to route both runtime input and runtime state through ccbot core, using ccbot ontology/control-plane semantics instead of ad-hoc supervisor scraping.

Important boundary:

- `ccbot send` is Telegram delivery surface (`ccbot send --help` now exposes that CLI) and must remain text/file delivery to Telegram.
- Runtime/TUI input needs a separate ontology-aware command/API, not `ccbot send`.

## Proposed work

Add a ccbot core runtime-control CLI/API for external supervisors, e.g. `ccbot inject`, `ccbot input`, or `ccbot runtime-send`, plus a state/status query if one does not already exist.

The command/API should:

- resolve the target through existing ccbot state/ontology where possible (`CCBOT_DIR`, surface/window binding, runtime kind, tmux window id);
- use existing live runtime input-plane checks and fail closed when no writable runtime plane is attached;
- reuse existing `RuntimeInputDriver` / `TmuxManager` paths, including Codex multiline `paste-buffer -p -d` + bare `Enter` semantics;
- provide machine-readable output suitable for supervisors (`--json`), including status such as `input_ready`, `busy`, `blocked_prompt`, `read_only`, `no_live_input_plane`, and target/window metadata;
- keep `ccbot send` documented and tested as Telegram delivery, not runtime injection.

## imm_supervisor consumer need

After this lands, `/home/tools/imm/scripts/imm_tmux_supervisor.sh` should stop scraping pane tails for state and stop embedding tmux prompt delivery. It should ask ccbot for runtime state and call the new ccbot runtime-input command when ccbot says the target is writable/input-ready.

## Acceptance criteria

- New ccbot runtime-input/status command or documented API exists and has `--help`.
- `ccbot send --help` remains Telegram delivery help and tests prove it is not runtime injection.
- Runtime input path uses ccbot ontology/live-input-plane guardrails and fails closed on read-only/no live plane.
- Codex multiline prompts use existing paste-buffer + bare Enter behavior through ccbot, not duplicated supervisor code.
- Tests cover CLI contract and at least one no-live-plane/read-only failure.
- Docs/ontology mention the boundary between delivery surface (`ccbot send`) and runtime input plane.

title: Ignore image ingress in unbound Telegram topics
description: If a Telegram topic/thread is not in the active bound runtime state, image-equivalent ingress should be silent instead of producing bind/unbound guidance or other visible bot reactions. Scope: incoming photo_handler images and sticker_handler image-equivalent stickers/thumbnails. Do not change ccbot send, runtime-input, inject, text, or command bind flows.
acceptance_criteria: Unbound/non-bind photo messages do not download files, do not call session_manager.send_to_window, do not call safe_reply, and do not open/mutate bind flow. Unbound/non-bind sticker messages follow the same silent behavior. Bound photo/sticker delivery behavior remains unchanged. Regression tests cover unbound photo and sticker ingress.
notes: GitHub issue https://github.com/six-ddc/ccbot/issues/84 was created before the request was clarified to Beads; keep the canonical task here.

title: Generated image artifacts are not delivered back to Telegram chat
description:
## Problem
A coding agent generated an improved sticker/image artifact with `gpt-image-2`, but the result stayed only as a local file and was not delivered back into the Telegram chat.

Observed example from @ImmArenaBot flow:
- Tool output reported: `Generated Image`
- Saved file: `/home/tools/imm/.codex/generated_images/019de621-9462-7bd0-b2b6-b0b5fe814107/ig_07505510d95b59320169f5ace6313881919be01593ef043a65.png`
- The Telegram chat did not receive the generated image/photo.

## Expected behavior
When an agent creates a final user-facing image artifact in an IMM/ccbot-controlled runtime, the result-delivery path should make it easy/reliable to send that artifact back to the originating Telegram surface, for example via the documented `ccbot send --file-path ... --file-type photo|animation` contract.

## Actual behavior
The generation output is visible in the runtime transcript as a local path, but no Telegram delivery happens. The user has to notice the path manually or ask for follow-up delivery.

## Boundaries
- Do not repurpose `ccbot send`; it remains outbound Telegram delivery.
- Do not change runtime/TUI input semantics (`runtime-input`/`inject`).
- Preserve topic/thread binding and CCBOT_DIR resolution guardrails.
- Do not print or expose Telegram tokens/secrets in diagnostics.

## Initial hypothesis
This is likely a missing integration/contract between coding-agent image generation artifacts and ccbot result delivery, not sticker ingress. The artifact exists, but no automatic or agent-friendly delivery hook invoked `ccbot send` for the originating chat/thread.
acceptance_criteria: A generated image artifact from an IMM/ccbot runtime can be delivered to the originating Telegram thread without manual path-copying; tests/docs cover the contract; ccbot send remains Telegram delivery only.

title: Continue ccbot desloppify cleanup beyond current small batch
description: Follow-up from 2026-05-02 cleanup pass. Current desloppify status after commits: strict 79.0/100, 251 open, focus Cross-module arch 68.0, queue/planned review work remains. Top remaining items include MessageTask variant bag, SessionManager state hub, callback window authorization binding checks, delivery package placement, and audit API width. Keep future batches small, behavior-locked with tests, and avoid mixing production behavior fixes with broad cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Run desloppify next/show review, choose one bounded item, add/keep regression coverage, run targeted tests plus ruff, rescan, and record resolved issues with desloppify plan commit-log.
